# NCERT Summary: International Business II

## Chapter at a Glance

This chapter deals with the expansion of business beyond national borders, focusing on various aspects of international trade and investment. It covers the concept of international business, types of international business, and the factors influencing its growth. Additionally, the chapter discusses the role of various international business organizations, such as the World Trade Organization (WTO), International Monetary Fund (IMF), and the World Bank. The chapter also highlights the importance of international marketing, transportation, and logistics in the expansion of international business.

## Section-wise Summary

### Concept of International Business

International business refers to the activities of business enterprises that operate across national borders. It involves the exchange of goods, services, and capital between countries. International business can be classified into two categories: international trade and international investment. International trade involves the exchange of goods and services between countries, while international investment involves the flow of capital from one country to another.

### Types of International Business

There are two main types of international business: (i) Export-based businesses and (ii) Import-based businesses. Export-based businesses are those that export goods and services from one country to another, while import-based businesses are those that import goods and services from one country to another.

### Factors Influencing International Business

Several factors influence the growth of international business, including (i) Economic factors, such as GDP, inflation, and interest rates; (ii) Political factors, such as government policies, trade agreements, and international relations; (iii) Social factors, such as culture, language, and education; and (iv) Technological factors, such as advancements in transportation and communication.

### International Business Organizations

Several international business organizations play a crucial role in promoting and regulating international trade and investment. These organizations include (i) World Trade Organization (WTO), which aims to promote free trade and reduce trade barriers; (ii) International Monetary Fund (IMF), which provides financial assistance to countries facing economic difficulties; and (iii) World Bank, which provides financial and technical assistance to developing countries.

### International Marketing

International marketing involves the promotion of goods and services across national borders. It requires a deep understanding of the cultural, social, and economic factors that influence consumer behavior in different countries. International marketing can be achieved through various channels, including advertising, sales promotion, and public relations.

### International Transportation and Logistics

International transportation and logistics play a critical role in the expansion of international business. It involves the movement of goods and services from one country to another, which requires a efficient and reliable transportation system. International logistics involves the management of the flow of goods, services, and information across national borders.

## Important NCERT Exercises Insight

The NCERT exercises for this chapter test the students' understanding of various concepts related to international business, including (i) the concept of international business; (ii) types of international business; (iii) factors influencing international business; and (iv) international business organizations. The students should remember the key concepts tested in the exercises, including (i) the importance of international trade and investment; (ii) the role of WTO, IMF, and World Bank; and (iii) the significance of international marketing, transportation, and logistics.

## NCERT Exemplar Focus

The NCERT Exemplar for this chapter covers additional topics related to international business, including (i) the impact of globalization on international business; (ii) the role of multinational corporations in international business; and (iii) the challenges faced by international businesses in emerging markets. The students should focus on these topics to gain a deeper understanding of international business.

## Board Exam Relevance

The board exam questions for this chapter typically test the students' understanding of various concepts related to international business, including (i) the concept of international business; (ii) types of international business; and (iii) factors influencing international business. The marks weightage for this chapter is around 10-15% of the total marks. The frequently asked topics from this chapter include (i) the role of WTO, IMF, and World Bank; (ii) the importance of international marketing, transportation, and logistics; and (iii) the challenges faced by international businesses in emerging markets.
